08 Stakeholder Review - YouTube.mp4
It's rare that you can actually get everyone in the same room for a value engine mapping meeting, which is why it is critical to have all value engine drafts reviewed by any missing stakeholders before they're finalized. If everyone is in the same office, this process is pretty simple, right? You can just bring them into the room where the value engine is still posted on the whiteboard. If you're not in the same office, then a picture should suffice. Literally, just snap a picture with your iPhone and send it to them. Either way, the question you ask to kick off the stakeholder review meeting is always the same. What are you currently doing that is not represented on this flowchart? In other words, what did we miss? Now, in a perfect world, they say, nothing. This looks great, and you move on. If they do notice a missing stage or activity, then you're gonna need to determine if it is truly missing a major step, or if they're simply describing a sub-step of an action or activity that's already represented somewhere else on the value engine. Now, if you determine that it's a major missing step— added in— right?
 Added in and inform everyone who was in the first meeting of this change. Now, I'm not going to lie. This can be mildly annoying because you're going to have to erase a lot of lines and shift things around. But believe me, it's worth it to get things right, even if you're a little bit OCD like me. If you do determine it to be a sub-step, then do this: take the time to explain to the team member how their role fits into the existing stage and into the value engine as a whole. I promise both you and your team member will benefit from the added context and the added trust that's built by taking just a little bit of time to clearly articulate their value to this critically important process and to the company as a whole. But again, err on the side of simplicity. You don't need to represent each and every step in the process just to make everybody happy. That's a job for the checklists and playbooks that we're going to be building in a future session. For now... Keep it simple. But take the time to ask, what did we miss?
 And when you do that, take the time to educate your team members on how the work they do factors into the broader value creation process of the company.
